channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: IMF Warns Iran Conflict Could Raise Global Prices and Slow Economic Growth
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has issued a warning that the ongoing war in the Middle East, triggered by US and Israeli strikes on Iran on February 28, 2026, is causing serious disruptions to ...

item.title: Health Minister engages stakeholders amid legal challenges to NHI Act
item.description: Health Minister Aaron Motsoaledi has recently met with the South African Medical Association (Sama) to explore common ground regarding the National Health Insurance (NHI) Act, which is facing multiple...

item.title: UP GE Club rebrands national geodetic summit to foster collaboration in engineering and geomatics
item.description: The University of the Philippines Geodetic Engineering Club (UP GE Club) is celebrating a milestone with the launch of the National Geodetic Engineering Summit (NatGES) 2026. The two-day event, happen...

item.title: Greenpeace warns of humanitarian and nuclear risks if Iran’s power grid is attacked
item.description: Greenpeace International has strongly condemned former President Donald Trump's threats to target Iran's electricity infrastructure, emphasizing that such actions could provoke a severe humanitarian c...

item.title: Canadian Officials Alert Public About Fraud Risks Ahead of FIFA World Cup
item.description: Canadian authorities are issuing warnings about potential fraud and scams as the FIFA World Cup nears, urging citizens to be vigilant and cautious. The upcoming international tournament, which draws l...

item.title: Governor Sakaja Rejects Arrest, Calls for Dialogue Between Senate and Governors
item.description: Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja has publicly rejected the arrest order issued by Inspector General Douglas Kanja, describing it as unnecessary and a form of unwarranted drama. Sakaja explained that th...

item.title: How long-term fugitives manage to stay hidden from Australian police
item.description: The article analyses how long-term fugitives such as Dezi Freeman manage to evade police despite modern technology and extensive resources. Freeman, who killed two police officers in Victoria in Augus...

item.title: Reflections on Nowruz Amid Joy, Memory, and Concern for Iran
item.description: Rana Pishva reflects on the personal and cultural significance of Nowruz, the Iranian New Year, as she celebrates in Ottawa. Growing up, her family marked the occasion with symbolic foods such as waln...

item.title: Harnessing Geothermal Energy as a Key Philippine Energy Resource
item.description: The Philippines, with over 20 active volcanoes and more than 40 potentially active ones, has a significant potential to expand its geothermal energy production. Geologists study volcanoes not only for...

item.title: Omar Abdullah opposes amendment to walnut felling ban, calls it vital for Kashmir’s economy and heritage
item.description: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah defended the 57-year-old law that prohibits the felling of walnut trees during a legislative assembly session on Monday. The debate arose after National ...

item.title: Study suggests some heart attack survivors may safely discontinue long-term beta-blockers
item.description: For decades, beta-blockers have been prescribed as a standard lifelong treatment for adults who survive a heart attack, intended to reduce the risk of future cardiovascular events. These medications h...

item.title: Transport sector braces for fare hikes amid fuel price surge
item.description: South African taxi associations are planning to increase fares in response to a sharp rise in fuel prices set to take effect on 1 April. The move comes as commuters already struggle with the high cost...

item.title: Union Home Minister claims significant decline in Maoist activities in India
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah informed the Lok Sabha on Monday that India has effectively become free from Maoist violence. He stated that the government had set a deadline of March 31 to eliminate Ma...

item.title: Cebu senior citizen claims P100 million Ultra Lotto 6/58 jackpot after 8 days
item.description: A senior citizen from Cebu City successfully claimed the P100,331,631.91 jackpot prize in the Ultra Lotto 6/58 draw held on March 17. The winner correctly guessed the numbers 29-52-49-55-25-09 and per...

item.title: Dubai’s glossy image faces collapse as war exposes deeper inequalities and exploitation
item.description: Brigid Delaney’s article examines how the image of Dubai, long polished by Western influencers and expats, is collapsing under the weight of geopolitical realities and moral contradictions. As conflic...

item.title: Former Tanzanian President Kikwete Meets South African President Ramaphosa on SADC and AU Matters
item.description: On Sunday in Pretoria, South Africa, the President of the Republic of South Africa, Cyril Ramaphosa, who also serves as the SADC Chairperson and Chairperson of the African Union’s Ad-Hoc High-Level Co...

item.title: Why the US is unlikely to militarily secure the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The article explains why the United States is unlikely to use force alone to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz for commercial shipping. It says Iran’s geographic position gives it a major advantage, b...

item.title: Tanzania Mining Commission urged to improve safety and environmental oversight in mining operations
item.description: In Mwanza, the Permanent Secretary of the Ministry of Minerals, Eng. Yahya Samamba, has called upon the Tanzania Mining Commission (TMC) officials, especially Resident Mining Officers, to increase the...

item.title: Sindh Government Declares Holiday on April 4 to Commemorate Zulfikar Ali Bhutto
item.description: The Sindh provincial government has officially declared a public holiday on Saturday, April 4, in memory of former Prime Minister and Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) founder Zulfikar Ali Bhutto. Accordin...

item.title: How Lost Crypto Keys Complicate Inheritance for Older Australians
item.description: A growing number of older Australians are investing in cryptocurrencies, but traditional estate planning systems are struggling to manage these digital assets after their owners pass away. The article...
